title: feat: migrate Sana Sprint 1.6B to GH200
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Sana hits the turbo button
gist.blurb: We've upgraded our Sana model to the 1.6B version and moved it to blazing-fast GH200 hardware. Enjoy higher quality images generated in a fraction of a second!
gist.summary: Upgraded the default Sana image generation model to 1.6B and migrated its infrastructure to faster GH200 instances.
gist.impact: Users will notice higher quality image generation and significantly faster response times, dropping to just ~0.165s per image.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee as an engineer upgrading a server rack with a glowing new GPU. The monitor robot companion holding a stopwatch to represent the speed increase. A '1.6B' label on the server.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny engineer's hard hat, installing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) GPU into a retro server rack labeled '1.6B'. The chunky monitor-head robot stands nearby holding a pixelated stopwatch showing a very fast time. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny engineer's hard hat, installing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) GPU into a retro server rack labeled '1.6B'. The chunky monitor-head robot stands nearby holding a pixelated stopwatch showing a very fast time. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
